reviewer: Claude native-size reference review (honest-heading + feedback-legibility pass: velocity-aligned yaw with fishtail slides, skid rubber/smoke aftermath, coral/white damage flash, gold glory shimmer, edge pulses, persistent smashed-barrier wreckage; 13-beat montage re-inspected at 160x360 against both references plus 5x crops of the new drift and wreck beats and rendered-clip frames)

characterCraft.note: The reference-scale constructed vehicles hold: getaway coupe/taxi 10x18, muscle 11x18, cruiser 11x19, interceptor 11x17, police bike 5x16, police van 13x21, traffic 8x12-17, pedestrians 3x9, helicopter 31px airframe, each with nose highlight, cabin glass, visible wheelman, wheels, and per-kind signatures. New character truth on the body itself: the silhouette now points where the car actually travels (the old wrong-signed crab lean is gone), committed slides read as a genuine fishtail with the tail swung past the travel line, damage blinks the whole body coral/white, a fresh escape sweeps a gold rim light, and a hurting car trails tiered smoke from 30 damage up with embers past 70.

environmentCraft.note: The five-district city keeps its layered build — wet neon avenue with cats-eyes and steaming manholes, market tram street, canal water and boardwalks, port rails and gantry, civic roundabout, curb refuges, skyline, foreground lights — and the road now records the chase itself: teal-lit skid rubber fades on the world clock where slides happened, and a smashed roadblock leaves knocked-flat barriers with a denser punch-through hole instead of evaporating. Aftermath is world state the viewer can read a second later.

levelVariety.note: The thirteen-beat sheet shows five structurally distinct districts: Neon Core boulevards, Old Market tram streets, Canal Ward waterways, Iron Port rails and gantries, Civic Crown roundabouts. Landmarks, street grammar, side silhouettes, and traversal fixtures change rather than merely swapping palette; structure-distance floors still hold across every district pair.

animationImpact.note: Aligned crops keep proving motion (player and police bursts above floors), the ramp keeps its takeoff-apex-landing arc with gold launch and landing puffs, and the swap stages old car to running driver to new car with the GO! beat. New at native size and proven with same-sim FX-ablated twins: the handbrake slide fishtails while rubber and smoke pour off the rear wheels (drift beat, 0.020 changed fraction in the trail crop), and the roadblock crunch flashes the body, throws coral debris and white barrier chunks, scrubs speed 2.2 to 0.95, and pulses the screen edge coral (wreck beat, 0.035 changed). The dragnet still assembles physically and wrecked units limp under stun sparks.

readability.note: Intent reads from the driver alone, and now honestly: the nose tracks actual travel with yaw inertia, so a lane change reads as a committed diagonal and a slide reads as a caught fishtail — never the old permanent crab. Every good/bad beat is tagged in one glance: gold glory shimmer and gold edge pulse on escapes, coral/white body flash and coral edge pulse on damage, mint ticks on threads and near-misses, skid and wreck aftermath persisting where it happened. Executable feedback-legibility gates (evals/feedback.js) prove all thirteen event categories render palette-separated pixels at the event location. Wanted stars, heat/damage bars, run-progress strip, escape pips, tactic text, and police formations stay legible beside video with ZERO drawn guidelines.

artDirectionCohesion.note: Neon-noir ink, cyan and magenta pursuit lighting, warm district accents, cream pixel construction, restrained HUD, physical warning grammar, and the new feedback language stay one voice: gold means won, coral means hurt, teal rubber means the tires worked. The rescaled actors, architecture, tactics, aftermath decals, and celebrations read as one SIDE/QUEST world.

guidelineOverlays.note: Checked all thirteen sampled beats at native size: no route lines/dots, arrows, alley/ramp/swap target highlights, police intercept predictions, ghost phantom cars, predicted arcs, or safe-lane markers anywhere. Skid rubber and wreck debris are AFTERMATH of committed motion, never a preview of it. The dragnet warning stays a physical world telegraph plus broadcast tint; the helicopter spotlight is world light from a visible aircraft. Executable pixel gates (opposite forced plans, injected ghost decoy) render identically at every planning beat.
